tch_cascade_hgb_ablation.description: TCH cascade — HGB ablation (TCH-Combined vs TCH-Lite). All other components identical.

single_pipeline_channel_ablation.description: Single-pipeline channel ablation on memorygraph_v2_sota_nw080 (from prior Phase C work)
single_pipeline_channel_ablation.split: phase-c-channels (2940 windows; older split — predictions cached in archive/)

interpretation.tch_hgb_ablation: Removing the numeric-feature triage gate (HGB) collapses triage PR-AUC strict from 0.9998 to 0.3101 (-69% rel). All retrieval (Hit@1/Hit@5/MRR) and novelty metrics are bit-identical because the L2 retrieval fusion and L3 novelty layers do not consume HGB output. This is the deployability cost of running without numeric telemetry.
interpretation.channel_ablation: On the memorygraph SOTA retrieval pipeline, masking trace-style and k8s-event lines from memory text produces IDENTICAL predictions to the full-channel baseline (0/2940 windows changed). Only log-line masking changes predictions — 1285/2940 windows (44%) see a different top-1 candidate; Hit@5 drops 7.8% rel and Hit@1 drops 19% rel. This indicates that log lines carry essentially all the retrieval signal on the memory side; trace and k8s lines as text content are noise the retriever does not index against.
interpretation.combined_implication: TCH-Lite (drop HGB + log-only memory) is the deployable-without-tracing configuration. The cascade-level cost is purely on the triage axis (PR-AUC drops ~70% rel); retrieval and novelty channels are preserved because (a) the L2/L3 layers do not depend on HGB and (b) the memory-side text channels other than logs were already contributing nothing to retrieval, so removing them is free.

limitations: Single-pipeline channel ablation is measured on memorygraph SOTA, not on the full TCH cascade. The cascade composes 4 retrievers (BiEncoder, Hybrid-RRF rule, LogSeq2Vec, KG); a full cascade-level channel ablation requires re-running each retriever with masked text on both sides (memory + window). Estimated cost: 2-4 hours of GPU work.

The Phase C ablation runs on a 2940-window older split that includes more borderline / noise windows than the v2-resplit 1008-window test split. Absolute Hit@K numbers therefore are not directly comparable to the cascade headline; only the relative deltas within the same pipeline are meaningful.
Window-side text masking is not implemented in build_window_query_text yet. The Phase C masking is memory-side only. A symmetric query-side mask is a future enhancement.
